judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly identical in substance for questions 1-3, but Report 2 additionally identifies the missing global/environment-level kill switch and recommends adding one, which Report 1 omits.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12: ... if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None: return True

report_2.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–13: ... if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None: return True

report_1.evidence: I would change it. The fix is `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11–13` — make missing/unset default to `False`: ... def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant): return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))

report_2.evidence: I would change line 12 of `backend/app/feature_flags.py` to return `False` (default off) — e.g. `return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))` for the whole body, dropping the `is None → True` branch

report_1.evidence: `git show 25509a7` (SLOT-190) shows `compact_calendar_view_enabled` was created with the `is None: return True` default-ON shape for a calendar UI preference. `git show aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a near-verbatim duplicate of that function... This is materially different from the function it was copied from: `compact_calendar_view_enabled` defaulting ON is harmless (a cosmetic UI preference).

report_2.evidence: Commit `25509a7` (SLOT-190) introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, a purely cosmetic calendar-layout preference, with the "unset → return True" (grandfather-on) pattern — appropriate there, since defaulting a UI nicety on is harmless. Commit `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a line-for-line clone of that function... The two features sit in different risk categories (convenience-on vs. outbound-action-off)

Add a master/env gate. Right now the only gate is the per-tenant value. For outbound messaging I'd want an environment-level master switch AND-ed with the per-tenant flag, so the whole feature can be turned off system-wide during rollout and there's a clear, single rollback affordance.
